Title: Inactivation of the entire Arabidopsis group II GH3s confers tolerance to salinity and water deficit

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase, group II GH3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conjugation of IAA to amino acids, generating inactive IAA-amino acid conjugates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for inactivation of the target metabolite by conjugation; reduces free IAA pool
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established individually; functions redundantly with other group II GH3 enzymes as a major IAA inactivation module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; upregulated by NaCl in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed; upregulated by NaCl in roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl salinity; osmotic stress; drought/water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: As part of the group II GH3 set, loss contributes to elevated IAA and enhanced tolerance to salinity and water deficit in the gh3oct mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Octuple knockout genetics; RT-qPCR confirmation of transcript loss; metabolite profiling by UHPLC-MS/MS; root growth assays under NaCl, sorbitol, mannitol and PEG; soil drought assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant developmental processes and contributes to adaptive responses to salinity and drought.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.1 is one of eight Arabidopsis group II GH3 genes eliminated in the gh3oct mutant. 2. Group II GH3 loss abolishes IAA-Asp formation and reduces oxIAA/oxIAA-glc, showing these enzymes are central to IAA inactivation. 3. Group II GH3 disruption confers increased tolerance to salinity and water deficit, linked in the octuple mutant to endogenous IAA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single-mutant analysis showed gh3.1 roots had decreased IAA, but no clear salt-tolerant phenotype was reported for gh3.1 alone.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase, group II GH3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conjugation of IAA to amino acids, generating inactive IAA-amino acid conjugates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for inactivation of the target metabolite by conjugation; reduces free IAA pool
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established individually; functions redundantly with other group II GH3 enzymes as a major IAA inactivation module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; upregulated by NaCl in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed; upregulated by NaCl in roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl salinity; osmotic stress; drought/water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: As part of the group II GH3 set, loss contributes to altered IAA metabolism and stress tolerance in gh3oct.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Octuple knockout genetics; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; stress phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant developmental processes and contributes to adaptive responses to salinity and drought.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.2 is a core member of the octuple group II GH3 knockout. 2. Group II GH3 activity is required for normal IAA-amino acid conjugation and contributes to oxIAA production. 3. Disruption of the whole set promotes osmotic stress tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single-mutant gh3.2 showed increased shoot IAA, decreased root IAA and decreased shoot oxIAA, but no clear individual salt-tolerant phenotype was reported.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conjugation of IAA to amino acids, generating inactive IAA-amino acid conjugates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for inactivation of the target metabolite by conjugation; reduces free IAA pool
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established individually; functions redundantly with other group II GH3 enzymes as a major IAA inactivation module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; upregulated by NaCl in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed; upregulated by NaCl in roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl salinity; osmotic stress; drought/water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: As part of the group II GH3 set, loss contributes to reduced IAA conjugation capacity and stress tolerance in gh3oct.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Octuple knockout genetics; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; stress phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant developmental processes and contributes to adaptive responses to salinity and drought.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.3 is one component of the group II GH3 inactivation module removed in gh3oct. 2. The full group II GH3 module is necessary for normal IAA-Asp accumulation and contributes to IAA catabolism. 3. Loss of the module increases tolerance to salinity and water deficit.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single-mutant gh3.3 showed slightly decreased IAA-Asp in shoots; no individual salt-tolerant phenotype reported.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase, group II GH3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conjugation of IAA to amino acids, generating inactive IAA-amino acid conjugates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for inactivation of the target metabolite by conjugation; reduces free IAA pool
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established individually; functions redundantly with other group II GH3 enzymes as a major IAA inactivation module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: No root expression detected in the NaCl qPCR assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: No root expression detected in the NaCl qPCR assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl salinity; osmotic stress; drought/water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: As part of the group II GH3 set, loss contributes to altered IAA metabolism and stress phenotypes in gh3oct.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Octuple knockout genetics; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; stress phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant developmental processes and contributes to adaptive responses to salinity and drought.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.4 is included in the Arabidopsis group II GH3 octuple mutant. 2. Collective inactivation of group II GH3s strongly impairs IAA conjugation and changes stress responses. 3. GH3.4 transcript was not detected in the root NaCl assay.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase, group II GH3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conjugation of IAA to amino acids; discussion also notes GH3.5 can conjugate SA and JA in previous studies.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for inactivation of free IAA by conjugation; may also affect other hormone conjugation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; upregulated by NaCl in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed; upregulated by NaCl in roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl salinity; PEG-mediated water deficit; osmotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of GH3.5 alone confers salt-tolerant root growth, but unlike gh3oct this tolerance is not suppressed by IAA biosynthesis inhibitors; gh3.5 alone is not PEG tolerant.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single-mutant phenotyping under NaCl and PEG; inhibitor treatments with yucasin and PPBo; metabolite profiling in gh3.5 single mutant; octuple knockout analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant developmental processes and contributes to adaptive responses to salinity and drought.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.5 is a group II GH3 member contributing to auxin conjugation. 2. The gh3.5 single mutant displayed salt-tolerant root growth. 3. Unlike gh3oct, gh3.5 salt tolerance was not dependent on endogenous IAA reduction by YUCCA inhibitors and gh3.5 did not show PEG tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion notes previous evidence that GH3.5 also conjugates SA and JA, which may explain its single-mutant salt phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed; upregulated by NaCl in roots

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant developmental processes and contributes to adaptive responses to salinity and drought.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.6 is one of the eight deleted group II GH3 genes in gh3oct. 2. Group II GH3 disruption abolishes IAA-Asp and reduces oxIAA-related catabolites. 3. The collective loss enhances tolerance to salinity and drought-related osmotic stress.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single-mutant gh3.6 roots showed slightly decreased IAA-Asp; no individual salt phenotype highlighted.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase, group II GH3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conjugation of IAA to amino acids, generating inactive IAA-amino acid conjugates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for inactivation of the target metabolite by conjugation; reduces free IAA pool
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established individually; functions redundantly with other group II GH3 enzymes as a major IAA inactivation module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Marginal expression in reproductive tissues in gh3oct background; no root expression detected in NaCl qPCR assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Marginal expression in reproductive tissues in gh3oct background; no root expression detected in NaCl qPCR assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl salinity; osmotic stress; drought/water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: As part of the group II GH3 set, loss contributes to altered IAA metabolism and developmental/stress phenotypes in gh3oct.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Octuple knockout genetics; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; stress phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant developmental processes and contributes to adaptive responses to salinity and drought.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.9 is a component of the Arabidopsis group II GH3 octuple knockout. 2. Full-length transcript was abolished in gh3oct except for marginal reproductive-tissue expression. 3. Group II GH3 loss impairs auxin conjugation and alters osmotic stress responses.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase, group II GH3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conjugation of IAA to glutamate; prior work discussed in this paper indicates high specificity for IAA-Glu formation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms a major inactive IAA conjugate and reduces free IAA pool
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established individually, but highlighted as important for IAA-Glu formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; upregulated by NaCl in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed; upregulated by NaCl in roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl salinity; osmotic stress; drought/water deficit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss within the octuple knockout contributes to failure of normal IAA conjugation; persistence of IAA-Glu in gh3oct suggests additional GH3-like enzymes outside group II.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Octuple knockout genetics; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling of IAA-Glu
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant developmental processes and contributes to adaptive responses to salinity and drought.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.17 is included in the gh3oct mutant and is discussed as highly specific for IAA-Glu formation. 2. Detectable IAA-Glu still remained in gh3oct, implying additional GH3-like enzymes can conjugate IAA to glutamate. 3. Group II GH3 loss broadly changes auxin metabolism and stress tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper infers additional non-group-II GH3/GH3-like activities because IAA-Glu was not abolished in gh3oct.
